subject: Re: Workstation configurations
body:
Hi Bob,

I'd go with approximately the average of both configurations if that's an 
option. The 24 GB 3090 card would have better chances of keeping up with 
400 movies/hour since it can fit 2–3 processes running in parallel. I'd 
also go with a 16 core CPU. A large local SSD is not required, unless you 
plan to store data locally. Warp's disk footprint during processing is tiny.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, September 25, 2020 at 1:37:24 AM UTC+2 reas...@ucsd.edu wrote:

> I was wondering if anyone had any feedback on two GPU workstation 
> configurations I'm considering for our Warp setup. I currently image with a 
> K2 with 400 frames and 50 fractions per movie on our Krios. Basically 
> wondering if the cheaper one will handle the load or if I should get the 
> more expensive version to be safe or if there is some other changes I 
> should make to the them.
>
> Thanks for your help,
>
> Bob Ashley
> Facility Manager UC San Diego
>
